definitions.text: Having a large physical extent from side to side.
examples: We walked down a wide corridor.
The inquiry had a wide remit.

definitions.text: away from or to one side of a given goal
examples: The arrow fell wide of the mark.
A few shots were fired but they all went wide.

definitions.text: A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score
